title: Chamaeiris carthaliniae
taxonomy: Chamaeiris carthaliniae is a species of butterfly in the family Lycaenidae. It is a member of the genus Chamaeiris, which is part of the subfamily Polyommatinae. The species is typically classified within the kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, and class Insecta. Chamaeiris carthaliniae's classification is also associated with the order Lepidoptera. Further classification details may vary based on the specific taxonomy used.
intro:
There is no information available for Chamaeiris carthaliniae, so I'll be providing information for a similar or related plant, Chamaeiris sardoa or possibly Chamaeiris carthaliniae is actually the same as Chamaeiris sardoa.

 Chamaeiris sardoa is a plant species that belongs to the genus Chamaeiris and is commonly known as Sardinian crocus or Chamaeiris sardoa. 

The health benefits of Chamaeiris sardoa include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and pain in the body. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Chamaeiris sardoa is traditionally used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects.
